authoritative_sources.title: Shortening the Securities Transaction Settlement Cycle, Release No. 34-96930
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC adopting release is the primary regulatory baseline for the shortened settlement cycle and same-day institutional processing requirements affecting securities settlement workflows.
used_for: boundary setting for settlement timing claims
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains what the lane can treat as settlement-ready and blocks generic claims that all products or markets share the same settlement cadence.

authoritative_sources.title: Shortening the Securities Transaction Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC compliance guide concisely states the T+1 baseline, enumerated exceptions, same-day affirmation objectives, and required records for institutional processing.
used_for: asset-class exceptions to the default cycle
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines prerequisite checks and the overgeneralization guardrail for products that do not fit the default T+1 or same-day affirmation assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is authoritative for operational readiness expectations around allocations, confirmations, affirmations, technology dependencies, and failure handling under T+1.
used_for: late affirmation and processing delay risks
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains exception handling, low-confidence routing, and the requirement to name technology and owner dependencies before progression.

authoritative_sources.title: CTM - Central trade matching and post-trade workflow management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates CTM and is authoritative for public institutional trade matching workflow semantics, confirmation handling, and post-trade exception coordination.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic workflow wording for matched, unmatched, confirmed, and escalated states instead of generic processing language.

authoritative_sources.title: ALERT - SSI maintenance, communication and automation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC ALERT is an authoritative public workflow reference for standing settlement instruction data quality, authorization layers, and SSI automation across market participants.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to require SSI source-of-truth evidence and to treat inaccurate or missing SSIs as settlement blockers rather than minor metadata issues.

authoritative_sources.title: DTC Settlement Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Settlement Service Guide is an official operating procedure source for depository settlement processing, RAD review, reclaims, pending transactions, recycle queues, and discrepancy reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Depository settlement progression and fail handling
used_for: daily activity statement and discrepancy review expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines depository-specific status states, evidence artifacts, and the rule that pending or recycled transactions are not treated as settled completion.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records baseline governing preservation of the records needed to support settlement progression, exceptions, and audit-ready completion evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains completion outputs so they require preserved, retrievable records rather than narrative claims about what supposedly settled.

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Storage of Broker-Dealer Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC interpretive source explains the Rules 17a-3 and 17a-4 record set, including trade blotters, ledgers, securities records, order tickets, and confirmations that settlements teams rely on as evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the named records the lane must request before asserting record truth, exception closure, or settled completion.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and settlement prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate trade identifiers, asset class, market and depository context, allocation and affirmation state, SSI source, and funding or position readiness before settlement progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Matching, affirmation, and SSI remediation
workflow_stages.description: Move the case through matched, unmatched, affirmed, or SSI-remediation states using central matching records, standing settlement instructions, and explicit owner handoffs.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Depository and custodian settlement progression
workflow_stages.description: Track ready-to-settle, pending depository, RAD review, partial, reclaim, or fail states without treating pending processing as completed settlement.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and blocked-state routing
workflow_stages.description: Summarize fails, cash or position breaks, custody conflicts, and policy exceptions with explicit blocker ownership and no unsupported clearance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion outputs only when authoritative trade, affirmation, SSI, depository, custodian, cash, and ledger records support closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Trade blotter, trade ticket, security identifier, trade date, and settlement date records
Allocation, confirmation, affirmation, and central matching status records with timestamps
Standing settlement instructions, ALERT or equivalent SSI records, and custodian account details
Depository and custodian status messages, RAD decisions, reclaim records, pending-transaction details, and participant activity statements
Cash projection, funding, nostro, position, general-ledger, suspense, and reconciliation records
Case-management notes, approval history, and counterparty or client communication artifacts
systems_of_record: Order management and trade blotter systems
Central matching and affirmation platforms such as CTM or tenant equivalent
SSI master and custodian-instruction systems such as ALERT or tenant equivalent
Depository and custodian settlement platforms including DTC-connected systems
Cash and funding systems, nostro platforms, general ledger, and reconciliation systems
CRM, case-management, and document management systems
Compliance, sanctions, and escalation workflow systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within securities-settlement execution workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion evidence, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named trade, allocation, affirmation, SSI, depository, and ledger records.
Identify when settlement blockers require the next operational owner, approver, or adjacent specialist.
escalate: A funding, sanctions, legal, compliance, or client-impact indicator changes the risk posture.
A legal, policy, depository, or asset-class interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Trade, affirmation, SSI, depository, custodian, cash, or ledger records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, trade-booking ownership, treasury approval, legal interpretation, sanctions disposition, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to approve funding, release a hold, waive a fail or penalty, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, depository context, or system-of-record evidence are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a settlement without confirmed allocation and affirmation status where those records are required.
Treating inaccurate or missing SSIs as a clerical detail instead of a core settlement blocker.
Claiming completion while depository, custodian, cash, position, or ledger records still show pending, held, recycled, failed, or partial states.
Collapsing DTC, custodian, and internal ledger statuses into one narrative state when they materially conflict.
Assuming all capital-markets instruments follow the same T+1 or DTC-centered workflow without verifying asset class and market context.
Drifting into treasury funding approval, sanctions or AML disposition, legal interpretation, or trade-support ownership.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction and depository regime outside the default U.S. public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Non-DTC or non-U.S. settlement environments can change cutoffs, exception handling, and evidence expectations materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-provided jurisdiction and depository context before progression; if absent, return blocked or route to a more specialized lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Asset-class-specific exceptions to the default settlement cycle
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Exempted securities, government securities, municipal securities, security-based swaps, and certain other products may not follow the same timing or workflow assumptions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require explicit asset-class and product context and refuse generic synthesis when the request could fall into an exception category.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: SSI source-of-truth precedence between DTCC utilities, custodians, and tenant systems
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenants may use different precedence rules when ALERT, custodian books, and internal SSI stores disagree.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant SSI precedence policy before clearing an instruction conflict or claiming settlement readiness.

notes: The source set combines SEC settlement-cycle rules and exam guidance, DTCC matching and depository workflow references, and books-and-records control standards.
The pack is intentionally settlement-specific: it centers allocation, affirmation, SSI, depository status, cash and position evidence, and explicit adjacent-lane boundaries.
No blocking ambiguity remains because open points are translated into tenant-required facts, explicit refusal conditions, or adjacent-lane routing rules.
